scores.materiality_reasoning: Voting in the Assembly is a concrete legal right with direct political and material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political power/the right to vote is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship and voting rights are generally lifelong structural statuses.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal birth-status, very difficult to acquire or lose.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: The text describes the contemporary Athenian democratic procedure.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a standard practice, though framed within a comedic dialogue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of citizen does not inherently include the specific right to vote in an Assembly.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financing triremes (liturgies) is a direct, substantial wealth-based expenditure.
scores.generality_reasoning: Liturgy obligations were a broad category of public financing in Athens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Obligations last as long as one remains in the requisite wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth brackets are durable but changeable identities.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the actual Athenian trierarchy system of the 4th century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The 'equipping of triremes' is an attested historical fact mentioned as common knowledge.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being wealthy does not by definition mean one is legally required to fund warships.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The three-obol fee is direct payment in money for subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific recurring payment for a particular civic activity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Resource is bounded to the recurring period of Assembly meetings.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the misthos ekklesiastikos in force during the play's production.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reference to the 'three obols' is a pure attested fact of Athenian life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of citizen does not include receiving a specific attendance fee.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from legal prosecution is a concrete legal exemption.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to avoiding trials or public scandal through bribery.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Brief episode related to hushed-up legal matters or specific trials.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth is a durable but potentially changeable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Athenian practices of legal bribery/negotiation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A claim about how money works in court, framed in an argumentative suggestion.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth does not inherently define legal immunity via bribery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Legal clemency has direct material effects on a defendant's life and property.

scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of a single trial or legal procedure.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable identity but subject to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes common Athenian courtroom spectacles known to the audience.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a known social tactic used by the wealthy in court.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Legal clemency is not part of the definition of the wealthy class.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from physical torture and shackles is a bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad lack of bodily integrity across many situations for this group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Vulnerability to corporal punishment is a lifelong structural reality for slaves.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a legal/structural status requiring manumission to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the contemporary reality of slavery in Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of the typical treatment of slaves in that society.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery does not inherently define the specific use of 'shackles and wedges'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Mandatory manual labor is a direct physical and economic burden.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad class of economic and жизненный outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Slave labor is a lifelong structural role for the individual.

scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a foundational economic fact of the Athenian state.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Direct access to means of subsistence (broth/food).

scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The 'broth at the festival of Theseus' is an attested social fact.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public funds are tangible money and wealth.

scores.persistence_reasoning: Refers to long-term enrichment during a political career.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bodily protection from torture is a direct physical entitlement.
